title: Fig. 2 in Sensitive, quantitative detection of Besnoitia darlingi and related parasites in intermediate hosts and to assess felids as definitive hosts for known and as-yet undescribed related parasite species
author: Schares, Gereon
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 2. Coccidia-specific PCR to confirm the presence of DNA of various parasite species used to test the analytic specificity of the BdanjoRT1 real-time PCR. (1) Besnoitia darlingi, (2) B. neotomofelis, (3) B. oryctofelisi, (4) B. besnoiti (Evora isolate), (5) B. bennetti (Texas), (6) B. tarandi (Bt-CA-Quebec1), (7) Toxoplasma gondii, (8) Hammondia hammondi, (9) Neospora caninum, (10) H. heydorni, (11) Cystoisospora felis, (12) C. rivolta, (13) C. burrowsi, (14) C. canis, (15) Sarcocystis cruzi and (16) Crytosporidium parvum. Presence of (17) Giardia duodenalis and (18) Tritrichomonas foetus DNA was shown by amplification using species or genus-specific primers, respectively. C, negative control; M, marker.
